**1. Mathematical Equations**

*   Z = 1 + B/v + C/v^2 + D/v^3 + ...  (1)
*   P = RT / (v - b)  - a / (v^2+ yv + eps)  (2)

**2. Graphs**

***Figure 2: Distillation curves for kerosene samples***

*   **Axes:** X-axis: Distillate Volume Fraction, %; Y-axis: Tboil, K
*   **Curves:**
    *   JP-8 [10]: Scattered squares
    *   Jet-A [10]: Scattered triangle
    *   S-8 [17]: Scattered circles
    *   S-8 curve at 83 kPa, adjusted for volume shift

***Figure 3: Distillation curves for diesel samples***

*   **Axes:** X-axis: Distillate Volume Fraction, %; Y-axis: Tboil, K
*   **Curves:**
    *   Diesel [23]: Scattered circles
    *   Diesel [24]: Scattered triangles

***Figure 4: Phase diagram for jet-A and diesel fuels***

* **Axes**: X-axis: Temperature (K); Y-axis: Pressure (atm)
* **Curves**:
    * Jet-A: Vapor and liquid curves
    * ERBS: Vapor and liquid curves
    * Premium Diesel: Vapor and liquid curves
* **Symbols**: * critical points, circles - bubble points, x dew points

**3. Tables**

*   (No tables are identifiable in the provided image excerpt.)

**4. Diagrams**

*   Figure 4 shows phase diagrams for Jet-A, ERBS, and Premium Diesel.
    * Each diagram plots pressure (y-axis) against temperature (x-axis). The diagrams show liquid and vapor regions separated by equilibrium curves.

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters**

*   83 kPa (Pressure for S-8 distillation curve)
*   v, B, C, D, R, T, P, a, b, y, eps, Z represent specific volume,   virial coefficients, ideal gas constant, temperature,  pressure, as cubic constants

title: On_Model_Design_of_a_Surrogate_Fuel_Formulation
text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 4] Here's a breakdown of the technical content extracted from the text: **1. Mathematical Equations:** * Z = 1 + f1(T)/V + f2(T)/V^2 + f3(T)/V^n + f4(T) * [(gamma1 + gamma2/V^2)/V]*exp(-gamma2/V^2) * p/rhoRT = Ipol/T + sum{i=1 to n} ni* expid/ epsilon *exp(-gamma^2) * Z = 1 - v*(d(A'/RT)/dv) * A'/RT = [A ideal(T,v)/RT] + sum [A tail (T,v)/RT] * A' = Aideal + Asegm + Achain + Aassoc * Psi_m = sum{i=1 to n} sum {j=1 to n} yi*yj * Psi_ij * Psi_ij = (Psi_ii + Psi_jj)/2 * (1-lij) * Psi_ij = sqrt(Psi_ii * Psi_jj) * (1 - kij) * P = RT/(v-b) - a/(v(v+b)) **2. Graphs/Charts:** NO_VISUAL_CONTENT **3. Tables:** | EOS | Advantage | Disadvantage | | :------------------------- | :------------------------------------------------------------ | :-------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Virial equation of state | • Relatively simple | • Not precise at high pressure • Coefficients are very difficult to determine • Usually can be applied only for a single-phase gas system | | Cubic | • Simple
• Analytic expressions for density or fugacity are not too demanding | • Adequate, but does not provide perfect accuracy • For polar and associating substances, four and more parameters should be used • Inapplicable for mixtures of fluids with big differences in molecular size | | BWR and MBWR | • Good precision | • Many parameters should be calculated • Results depend strongly on errors in experimental data used for the parameter evaluation • Generalized modifications (with a small amount of parameters) are not accurate | | Based on the perturbation theory (perturbed hard chain, statistical associated fluid, and perturbed hard chain of spheres) | • Good precision
• Applicable for mixtures of substances with big differences in molecular size
• Applicable for high pressures | • Very complicated • For mixtures of unlike molecules, three or four pure-component parameters are needed | **4. Diagrams:** NO_VISUAL_CONTENT **5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:** * 30: Maximum number of parameters in the function fi. * gamma1, gamma2: Additional parameters in equation (3). * v: molar volume. * I_pol, I_exp, T_i, dj: Variables defined in the trial and error procedure. * Z: Compressibility factor. * A': Perturbation term for the residual Helmholtz energy. * Aideal: Helmholtz energy of an ideal gas * Achain: Contribution from the molecules chains * Asegm: Contribution from molecule segments * Aassoc: contribution from associations of components. * Psi_ij, lij, kij: Parameters used in mixing rules. * R: gas constant * T: Temperature * P; Pressure * a, b: constants * SRK: Soave Redlich Kwong.

title: On_Model_Design_of_a_Surrogate_Fuel_Formulation
text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 5]
Here's a breakdown of the technical content extracted from the image:

**1. Mathematical Equations:**

*   ln(phi_i) = -ln(1 - (b_i/v)) + (b_i/b_mix)*(z - 1) + (a_mix/(b_mix*RT))*(b_i/b_mix)
*   K_i  =   (y_i / x_i)  =  (P_crit, i / P)  *  exp[ 5.42 ( 1 - (T_crit,i/T) ) ]
*   a_mix_L = sum(i=1 to N) sum(j=1 to N) x_i*x_j*a_ij
*   b_mix_L = sum(i=1 to N) x_i*b_i
*   a_mix_V = sum(i=1 to N) sum(j=1 to N) y_i*y_j*a_ij
*   b_mix_V = sum(i=1 to N) y_i*b_i
*   kappa_i = 0.48508 + 1.5571*omega_i - 0.15613*omega_i^2
*   alpha_i = [1 + (1 - sqrt(T/T_crit,i))^2]
*   a_i = 0.42747*(R^2 * T_crit,i^2)/P_crit,i
*   b_i = 0.08664*(R*T_crit,i)/P_crit,i
*   a_ij = sqrt(a_i * a_j)*(1 - k_ij)
*   f_i^V = y_i * phi_i^V * P = x_i * phi_i^L * P, for i = 1 to N
*   sum(i=1 to N) y_i = 1
*   F(1) = P - (RT)/(v - b_mix^L) - (a_mix^L)/(v*(v + b_mix^L))
*   F(2) = P - (RT)/(v - b_mix^V) - (a_mix^V)/(v*(v + b_mix^V))
*   F(3) = phi_i^L*x_i - phi_i^V*y_i
*  F(n+1) = phi^L_n-1 x_n-1 - phi^V_n-1 y_n-1
*  F(n+2) = phi^L_n x_n - phi^V_n (1 - sum(j=1 to n-1 y_j))
*  A-A_o = sum(i=1 to N) (del A / del n_i) Delta n_i + 1/2 sum(i=1 to N)sum(j=1 to N) (del^2 A/ del n_i del n_j)Delta n_i Delta n_j +1/6 sum(i=1 to N) sum(j=1 to N) sum(k=1 to N) (del^3 A/ del n_i del n_j deln_k) Delta n_i Delta n_j Delta n_k + O(Delta n^4)
* (del A/ del n_j)_T,v = RT (

title: On_Model_Design_of_a_Surrogate_Fuel_Formulation
text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 6]
Here's a breakdown of the technical content extracted from the provided image:

**1. Mathematical Equations:**

*   C = SUM_i SUM_j SUM_k (d^3A / dn_i dn_j dn_k) Delta_n_i Delta_n_j Delta_n_k = 0
*   Q_ij/RT = (delta_ij / b_mix) + (beta_i + beta_j)F_1 + beta_i beta_j F_1^2
*   (beta_i beta_j F_3 - a_ij / a_mix F_5) + (beta_i beta_j - alpha_i beta_j - alpha_j beta_i)F_6
*   alpha_i = (1/a_mix) SUM_j x_j a_ij,  beta_i = b_i / b_mix
*   Cn_T^2 / RT = SUM_i^N  ( Delta_n_i^3 / x_i^3) + 3N(beta_i F_1)^2 + 2(beta_i F_1)^3 + (a_mix / b_mix) (3* beta_i^2 (2 a_bar - beta_bar)) X times (F3 + F6) - 2 beta BAR F4 -3 beta BAR alpha BAR F6

*   N_BAR = SUM_i Delta_n_i, alpha_BAR = SUM_i Delta_n_i alpha_i
*   beta_BAR = SUM_i Delta_n_i beta_i, a_bar = (1 / a_mix) SUM_i SUM_j Delta_n_i Delta_n_j a_ij

*  F_1 = 1 / (K - 1), F_2 = 2 /(K + 1)
*  F_3 = 2 (1 / (K + 1))^2 , F_4 = 2 (1 / (K + 1))^3
* F_5 = 2 ln((K+1)/K), F_5 = F_2 -F_5, K = v /b_mix

* d(M x_i) / dt = -Vy_i

**2. Graphs/Charts:**

NO_VISUAL_CONTENT. (Only flowcharts, not graphs/charts).

**3. Tables:**

NO_VISUAL_CONTENT.

**4. Diagrams:**

*   **Figure 5(a): Phase Diagram Calculation Flowchart**
    *   Components and labels:
        *   Take P, Read x, Terit, Pcrit, w
        *   First Iteration? (Yes/No)
        *   Assume y0 (if First Iteration = Yes)
        *   Take previous solution as initial point (if First Iteration = No)
        *   Calculate F, J = Jacobian F
        *   Solve Delta x = - J^-1 F
        *   Are new values reasonable? (Yes/No)
        *   Divide Delta x by 2 (if Are new values reasonable? = No)
        *   x_new = x_old + Delta x (if Are new values reasonable? = Yes)
        *   Delta x < x tolerance and |F| < F tolerance (Yes/No)
        *   Print Results. Take next P (if Delta x < x tolerance and |F| < F tolerance = Yes)
*   **Figure 5(b): Critical Point Calculation Flowchart**
    * Components and labels:
        *   Read w, P, x
